mechanism_summary: I2S substitution copies in-range fixed-point operands seen at comparison sites back into the bluex field, preventing overflow to the error sentinel; havoc freely drives bluex bytes into the overflow range.
one_line: I2S steers bluex toward valid range, blocking PNG_FIXED_ERROR overflow branch

mechanism_summary: No CMP literal gates this loop-exit; I2S overhead reduces throughput relative to the data-volume needed to reach the loop guard's false exit, while naive's faster broad havoc builds the required glyph buffer volume.
one_line: I2S overhead outweighs benefit; havoc builds glyph buffer volume for loop-exit false branch

mechanism_summary: Naive's undirected havoc stumbles onto the rare DFont magic header; I2S floods the corpus with well-known SFNT/OpenType table tags (MATH, GPOS, GSUB) that pin u.tag on TrueTypeTag, starving the DFontTag switch arm.
one_line: I2S floods corpus with SFNT tags, starving rare DFontTag switch arm

mechanism_summary: The combining class CCC129 is derived from glyph property tables, not a byte literal; I2S has no operand to substitute and its per-execution overhead slows exploration, while naive's broad havoc discovers the required structured font content.
one_line: I2S overhead wasted on table-derived combining class; havoc finds CCC129 structure

mechanism_summary: I2S substitutes the Extended_Pictographic comparison operand into the input, biasing the loser toward satisfying the true-branch conjunct (ZWJ followed by pictographic); havoc produces ZWJ-terminal buffers that take the false branch.
one_line: I2S substitutes pictographic operand, satisfying true-branch conjunct; havoc reaches false branch

mechanism_summary: Ragel table dispatch uses no input-side CMP operand, so I2S substitution has nothing to substitute; naive's havoc arithmetic mutation freely perturbs category bytes into the state-machine path while I2S overhead misdirects cmplog's budget.
one_line: Ragel table dispatch needs havoc byte exploration; I2S has no operand to substitute

mechanism_summary: Syllable state-machine traversal requires long diversified category-byte runs; naive's havoc/token stack grows and diversifies input length freely, while I2S's per-CMP substitution adds cost and steers mutations away from the homogeneous runs the machine needs.
one_line: I2S overhead and misdirection starves the long category runs this state machine needs

mechanism_summary: Indic state machine needs dense glyph-category byte runs; I2S substitutes table-tag tokens (kern, ASCII fragments) steering loser away from the small repeated-byte patterns the Ragel machine needs, while naive havoc freely produces those runs.
one_line: I2S inserts table-tag tokens diverting from glyph-category byte runs; havoc produces dense category sequences

mechanism_summary: Gate is a font-derived glyph-category/config condition with no input-literal CMP operand; I2S substitution gains no leverage and adds overhead that diverts mutation energy from the structural exploration naive's havoc performs freely.
one_line: No input-literal CMP operand; I2S overhead misdirects from structural category exploration

mechanism_summary: Naive havoc stumbles onto the short Repha-leading syllable where limit-base<=2; I2S substitutions steer cmplog toward shaped-glyph position/category predicates that are not literal CMP operands, misdirecting mutations away from the rare short-syllable layout.
one_line: I2S misdirects from short-syllable structural layout needed for limit-base<=2

mechanism_summary: Gate is has_reph=true requiring a Repha-classified first glyph; I2S substitutes 'kern' and similar table tags that steer the corpus toward a different syllable region, missing the LOG_REPHA path that naive's broad havoc/token mutation reaches.
one_line: I2S substitutes table tags steering corpus away from Repha-classified syllable

mechanism_summary: I2S substitutes intercepted font-table-tag operands (kern, GPOS) back into inputs, fixating the cmplog corpus on table-name strings and steering it away from the 4-byte little-endian glyph records needed for POS_RA_TO_BECOME_REPH; naive havoc drifts there freely.
one_line: I2S substitutes table tags fixating corpus away from Reph glyph-record bytes

mechanism_summary: I2S copies script-tag CMP operands back into inputs, repeatedly biasing the buffer toward the Malayalam/Tamil true side; naive's undirected havoc/token mutation broadly samples the codepoint space and hits the common non-Malayalam/non-Tamil false branch instead.
one_line: I2S pins script-tag operands to Malayalam/Tamil, blocking the non-Tamil/Malayalam false branch

mechanism_summary: Gate is a post-shaping glyph-category predicate (is_halant) with no input-side CMP operand; I2S substitutions waste energy and steer corpus away from the structural diversity needed to produce a halant-bearing syllable that naive's havoc reaches.
one_line: Post-shaping category gate; I2S wastes budget, reducing structural diversity needed

mechanism_summary: Gate requires a multi-glyph Indic syllable starting with Ra classified POS_RA_TO_BECOME_REPH; I2S substitutions key to intercepted CMP operands that do not help synthesize the multi-glyph syllabic structure, diverting energy from the structural exploration naive's plain havoc performs.
one_line: I2S CMP operands misdirect from multi-glyph Ra-Halant syllabic structure

mechanism_summary: Case 3 requires long homogeneous category-byte runs driving the Ragel khmer state machine; I2S substitution targets value-equality CMP operands and breaks those homogeneous runs, while naive's repeat/copy/insert havoc operators extend and sustain them.
one_line: I2S breaks homogeneous runs the Ragel state machine needs; havoc extends them

mechanism_summary: K_Cat(H) is a Unicode-property-derived glyph category, not an input-literal CMP operand; I2S substitution has no operand to swap and its effort is wasted/misdirected, so naive's broad havoc byte mutation stumbles onto the Coeng-producing byte arrangement instead.
one_line: Unicode property category has no input literal; I2S has nothing to substitute

mechanism_summary: Myanmar Ragel state machine needs small low-valued repeated byte runs; I2S substitutes table-magic ASCII tokens that satisfy font-header comparisons elsewhere but never form the myanmar_category state-machine sequence, diverting loser away.
one_line: I2S inserts table-magic tokens blocking myanmar_category state machine; havoc produces low-byte runs

mechanism_summary: Ra/As/H trigraph is a table-derived category sequence with no literal CMP operand; cmplog wastes mutation energy on CMP-operand substitution and collapses onto a self-reinforcing byte motif, while naive freely explores glyph-category space and stumbles on the trigraph.
one_line: Table-derived Ra/As/H trigraph; I2S collapses corpus onto non-Ra byte motifs

mechanism_summary: The VBlw combining category is derived from parser tables, not a raw CMP operand; I2S substitution targets comparison-captured operands unrelated to the derived category, wasting budget, while plain havoc+token mutation explores the glyph-sequence space.
one_line: I2S targets wrong operands for parser-derived VBlw category; havoc explores glyph space

mechanism_summary: Havoc byte mutation preserves and extends a repeating 4-byte record stride, accumulating multiple VPre matras; I2S substitutes CMP-derived operands that derail the uniform multi-matra run, so naive wins by omission of I2S.
one_line: Havoc preserves periodic record run; I2S derails multi-matra accumulation

mechanism_summary: I2S rewrites input bytes toward comparison operands captured inside the shaper, but these come from derived codepoints not direct input fields, so I2S rewrites the wrong offsets; havoc randomly lands the 0x1B/0x1D/0x1F low byte next to 0x0E, satisfying the equality.
one_line: I2S substitutes wrong offsets for derived Thai codepoint; havoc lands 0x0E1B/D/F byte pair

mechanism_summary: SDL action is produced by internal state-machine table lookups with no input-vs-constant CMP; I2S has no literal operand to inject, and its per-CMP callback overhead slows raw byte exploration relative to naive's unencumbered havoc.
one_line: State-machine-derived action; I2S has no target, adds overhead, naive wins

mechanism_summary: RD action is reached by accumulating enough Thai glyphs through the state machine; byte-expansion havoc grows inputs to satisfy the quantitative sequence requirement, while cmplog's CMP-biased budget produces shorter inputs under-driving the machine.
one_line: Havoc byte-expansion builds long glyph sequence; cmplog shortens, missing RD

mechanism_summary: Havoc sequence-building mutators grow input to a long multi-glyph Thai sequence traversing the state machine to action==RD; I2S substitution targets fixed-offset CMP operands and does not build the longer glyph sequence, keeping cmplog blocked.
one_line: Havoc grows glyph sequence to action==RD; I2S misses structural growth

mechanism_summary: Case 11 dispatch is a category-table range lookup with no literal operand; I2S substitution provides no traction and crowds out the broad havoc glyph-category exploration that naive uses to stumble into the virama-terminated-cluster transition.
one_line: Havoc explores category sequences to case 11; I2S crowded out with no target

mechanism_summary: Glyph-category gate USE(H)/USE(HVM)/USE(IS) is computed from shaped buffer, not a direct input comparison; CMP buffer holds no operand mapping back to input offset, so I2S substitution cannot manufacture the needed category and per-execution CMP cost slows cmplog relative to naive's havoc.
one_line: Derived glyph-category equality; I2S cannot substitute, adds overhead, naive wins

mechanism_summary: Naive havoc plants the 0x3a ICMPv6 protocol byte by saturating header offsets; I2S substitutes CMP operands that steer toward non-ICMPv6 protocol values, misdirecting the corpus away from the kProtoIcmp6 switch case.
one_line: havoc plants 0x3a ICMPv6 byte; I2S substitutions steer away from it

mechanism_summary: Naive havoc saturates header bytes with 0x3a (ICMPv6 proto marker); I2S finds no productive CMP operand toward 0x3a and its substitution stages instead misdirect mutations away from the ICMPv6 proto check, making comparison-solving a net disadvantage.
one_line: havoc saturates header with 0x3a; I2S misdirects away from ICMPv6 proto byte
